methods.rationale: 23 same-sector peers exist in Space & Aerospace, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Bellatrix Aerospace is a pre-Series B space and aerospace company that has raised $8M to date. The valuation universe comprises 5 public comparables (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and 8 private peers including SpaceX, Rocket Lab, Blue Origin, Planet Labs, and Firefly Aerospace, representing a mature sector with established public benchmarks and well-capitalized private competitors.

Standard triangulation methods yield limited actionable results. Peer-median analysis is not applicable: while 23 same-sector peers exist, none of the narrow filters—same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar-valuation-tier—produced the minimum three datapoints required for a reliable median. Public-comp anchoring using the Space & Aerospace sector P/S median of 64.2x across the five public tickers cannot be applied without the target's revenue, which is not disclosed. The sector's maturity and heterogeneity across launch services, orbital infrastructure, and satellite operations further complicate direct comparability.

This analysis cannot support a specific valuation range without material disclosure on Bellatrix's revenue, growth trajectory, and capital efficiency relative to peers. The primary uncertainty is whether the target operates in a high-multiple subsegment (e.g., reusable launch or orbital infrastructure) or a lower-multiple segment. Future valuation work should prioritize revenue disclosure and clarification of the company's specific mission and addressable market within the broader aerospace sector.
